metadata.seed: I work and work and work but I can't seem to get ahead. The volume at work has gotten so bad that I can't keep up. I'm getting injured and no one cares. I can barely pay my bills and it feels like there is no end in sight.

patient.query: I work and work and work but I can't seem to get ahead. The volume at work has gotten so bad that I can't keep up. I'm getting injured and no one cares. I can barely pay my bills and it feels like there is no end in sight.

automatic_thoughts: there is no end in sight

neutral: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: I can see how overwhelming this situation feels for you, with the high volume of work and financial struggles. It's important to remember that it's okay to feel frustrated and helpless in these moments. Let's take a moment to discuss some strategies to help manage your stress and find a way forward together.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it is overwhelming. I just... every time I think about it, I feel this knot in my stomach. I can handle a lot, but it's like everything’s piling up, and I can’t breathe. It’s hard to focus on strategies when I’m just trying to keep my head above water.

patient.query: Sure, I can think of a time last month. I had a project that was due, and everything just felt like it was crashing down. I remember staring at my computer, and no matter how hard I tried, I just couldn’t get myself to start. I felt so lost and frustrated. It was like everyone else was moving forward, and I was stuck in place.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it's true that I feel really alone in this. I think a friend might tell me to just take a step back, maybe even lighten up on myself a bit. But it’s hard to hear that when I feel like I’m letting everyone down. I mean, it just feels like there’s this heavy weight on my shoulders, and I can’t shake it off.

automatic_thoughts: there’s a heavy weight on my shoulders

llm_response.response: I understand that you're feeling quite alone right now, and it can be tough to hear advice from others when we're in this kind of emotional state. It’s like if I don’t push through, everything will fall apart. I just wish I could find a way to ease some of this weight without feeling like I’m giving up.

patient.query: I think they’d probably say it’s okay to take a break, that everyone needs it sometimes. But in my mind, I can’t shake off this feeling that they’d think I’m not capable enough if I do. It makes me hesitate, you know? I feel like taking a step back would just confirm all my fears about not measuring up.

automatic_thoughts: they’d think I’m not capable enough
taking a step back would confirm my fears
